FHA Refinance

FHA refinance options in Florida

If you already have an FHA loan — or want to refinance into one — there are several FHA refinance options for Florida homeowners, each suited to a different goal: lowering your rate, tapping equity, or simplifying the process.

FHA Streamline Refinance

The fastest option for existing FHA borrowers. The FHA Streamline reduces paperwork, often skips a new appraisal, and is designed to lower your rate or payment with minimal hassle. No cash-out.

FHA Rate-and-Term Refinance

Refinance your current mortgage into a new FHA loan to change your rate or term — useful if rates have dropped or you want to move from an adjustable to a fixed payment.

FHA Cash-Out Refinance

Tap your home's equity for renovations, debt consolidation, or other needs. FHA cash-out lets eligible Florida homeowners borrow against equity, subject to FHA limits and a new appraisal.

When refinancing makes sense

Refinancing can pay off when rates fall, when you want to remove an adjustable rate, or when you've built equity and want to drop FHA mortgage insurance by refinancing into a conventional loan. We'll run the numbers so you know whether it's worth it.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What FHA refinance options are available in Florida?+
Three main types: FHA Streamline (fast, for existing FHA loans), rate-and-term (change your rate or term), and cash-out (borrow against equity).
Can I refinance to remove FHA mortgage insurance?+
If you've built roughly 20% equity, refinancing from FHA into a conventional loan is the common way to drop mortgage insurance. We can compare the costs and savings.
Do I need an appraisal to refinance an FHA loan?+
A rate-and-term or cash-out FHA refinance typically requires an appraisal. An FHA Streamline often does not.
How much equity do I need for an FHA cash-out refinance?+
FHA cash-out refinances have loan-to-value limits, so you'll need sufficient equity. We can review your numbers to see how much you could access.
